briefdescription.para: Array representing the transitions table of the FSM button.
Check if the button has been pressed.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call function port_button_get_pressed() and retrieve the status ✅ 2. Return the status

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_t struct than contains an fsm_button_t.

briefdescription.para: Check if the button has been released.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call function port_button_get_pressed() and retrieve the status ✅ 2. Return the inverse of the status

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_t struct than contains an fsm_button_t.

briefdescription.para: Check if the debounce-time has passed.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call function port_system_get_millis() and retrieve the current system tick ✅ 2. Check if the current system tick is higher than the field next_timeout ✅ 3. Return true if it is higher, otherwise return false

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_t struct than contains an fsm_button_t.

briefdescription.para: Store the system tick when the button was pressed.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call function port_system_get_millis() and retrieve the current system tick ✅ 2. Store it in the field tick_pressed ✅ 3. Update the field next_timeout considering the current tick and the field debounce_time_ms.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_t struct than contains an fsm_button_t.

briefdescription.para: Store the duration of the button press.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call function port_system_get_millis() and retrieve the current system tick ✅ 2. Update the field duration considering it and the field tick_pressed ✅ 3. Update the field next_timeout considering the current tick and the field debounce_time_ms

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_t struct than contains an fsm_button_t.

detaileddescription.para: This function initializes the default values of the FSM struct and calls to the port to initialize the associated HW given the ID.
This FSM implements an anti-debounce mechanism. Debounces (or very fast button presses) lasting less than the debounce_time_ms are filtered out.
The FSM stores the duration of the last button press. The user should ask for it using the function fsm_button_get_duration().
At start and reset, the duration value must be 0 ms. A value of 0 ms means that there has not been a new button press.

The user is required to reset the duration value once it has been read. Otherwise, this value may be misinterpreted by the user, if successive calls are made without having pressed the button. In such a case we would be reading past information. In order to reset the value, the function fsm_button_reset_duration() must be called.
In other words, the status flag of this FSM is the variable duration. A duration of 0 means that no new button has been pressed, a value other than 0 means that it has been pressed and the value is its duration, so it is the user's responsibility to clear this status flag.

The FSM contains information of the button ID. This ID is a unique identifier that is managed by the user in the port. That is where the user provides identifiers and HW information for all the buttons on his system. The FSM does not have to know anything of the underlying HW.

simplesect.para: Both unit and integration tests (example) are provided for this button library are available in this GitHub repository: https://github.com/sdg2DieUpm/simone/tree/simone_v1_test

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call the fsm_init() to initialize the FSM. Pass the address of the fsm_t struct and the transition table ✅ 2. Initialize the fields debounce_time_ms, button_id of p_fsm_button with the received values ✅ 3. Initialize the fields tick_pressed, duration of p_fsm_button with 0 ✅ 4. Call the function port_button_init()

detaileddescription.para: This function creates a new button FSM with the given debounce time and button ID.

simplesect.para: fsm_button_t* Pointer to the button FSM.

detaileddescription.para: This function destroys a button FSM and frees the memory.
This function is used to fire the button FSM. It is used to check the transitions and execute the actions of the button FSM.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call the fsm_fire() function. Pass the address of the fsm_t struct.

briefdescription.para: Return the duration of the last button press.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Retrieve and return the field duration

simplesect.para: uint32_t Duration of the last button press in milliseconds.

briefdescription.para: Reset the duration of the last button press.
Get the debounce time of the button FSM.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns the debounce time of the button FSM.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Retrieve and return the field debounce_time_ms

briefdescription.para: Check if the button FSM is active, or not.

detaileddescription.para: The button is inactive when it is in the status BUTTON_RELEASED.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Get the field current_state of the FSM (field f of the struct). ✅ 2. Return false if the current state is BUTTON_RELEASED. Otherwise, return true.
